steps.title: Convert given rates to calories per hour
steps.content:
The problem gives exercise rates in 30-minute intervals. To work with hours, multiply each rate by 2:
Hiking: $240 \times 2 = 480$ calories/hour
Bicycling: $180 \times 2 = 360$ calories/hour

steps.title: Calculate calories from 1 hour of bicycling
steps.content:
The adult has already completed 1 hour of bicycling:
$$1 \text{ hour} \times 360 \text{ calories/hour} = 360 \text{ calories}$$

steps.title: Set up and solve for hiking hours
steps.content:
Find the remaining calories needed, then solve for hiking time. Remaining calories: $2,760 - 360 = 2,400$. Let $h$ = hours of hiking. Then:
$$480h = 2,400$$
$$h = \frac{2,400}{480} = 5$$
This matches - D is correct.
